attractions.name: Acacia Park
attractions.description: Acacia Park is the oldest city park in Colorado Springs, established in 1871 by city founder General William Jackson Palmer. It serves as a central multi-use oasis in the heart of downtown, offering a mix of historic charm and modern amenities including a performance bandshell and a unique sculptural fountain. The park is a popular gathering spot for locals and visitors alike, hosting seasonal events like summer concerts and winter ice skating (Uncover Colorado, Visit Colorado Springs).
attractions.address: 115 E Platte Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 5:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.admission: Free (Park entry); Skate in the Park (Seasonal): $15 per person (includes skates), $2 discount for Active Duty Military & First Responders, Kids 4 and under Free with paid adult (Downtown Colorado Springs, PikeRide)
highlights: Uncle Wilber's Fountain: A unique musical sculptural fountain that operates during summer months (Uncover Colorado)
Skate in the Park: The city's only outdoor seasonal ice skating rink, open from November to January (Downtown Colorado Springs)
Ninja Warrior-style Playground: A modern, climbing-focused play structure for children (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
Story Coffee: A charming on-site "tiny house" coffee shop offering espresso and pastries (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
Acacia Park Bandshell: A historic stage used for free summer concert series and community events (Uncover Colorado)
Visitor Hub: On-site information center providing local guides and event schedules (Uncover Colorado)
attractions.tips: The park is best visited in summer for the fountain or winter for ice skating. Visitors can find parking in the nearby downtown garages or metered street parking. For the latest schedule of bandshell concerts and events, check the Downtown Partnership website or visit the park's own Visitor Hub (Visit Colorado Springs, Uncover Colorado).
attractions.nearby: Colorado Springs Pioneers Museum, U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Museum, Colorado Springs Fine Arts Center, Old Colorado City, Pikes Peak, Garden of the Gods (Uncover Colorado, Expedia)

attractions.name: Antlers Park
attractions.description: Antlers Park is a 3.4-acre historic downtown green space nestled beside the iconic Antlers Hotel, offering a quiet and scenic retreat in the heart of Colorado Springs. The park features open lawns and shaded walking paths with picturesque views of Pikes Peak, making it a popular spot for casual strolls and lunch breaks. It was originally designed as a landscaped arrival gateway for travelers arriving by train at the nearby Denver and Rio Grande Railroad depot in the early 1900s. (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.address: 31 W. Pikes Peak Ave. (W. Pikes Peak Ave. & Sierra Madre St.)
attractions.region: Downtown Colorado Springs (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun 5:00 AM - 10:00 PM (May 1 – Oct 31); Mon-Sun 5:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Nov 1 – Apr 30) (Colorado Springs City Website)
attractions.days_open: Open daily (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.admission: Free (Colorado Springs City Website)
highlights: Historic downtown location adjacent to The Antlers hotel (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
ADA-accessible dog park with separate areas for small and large dogs (Antlers Wyndham Hotel)
Large reservable group picnic shelter and outdoor picnic tables (Colorado Springs City Website)
Scenic views of Pikes Peak framed by mature shade trees (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
Paved sidewalk loop and walking paths for humans and pets (Trails and Open Space Coalition)
attractions.tips: Metered street parking and an adjacent parking garage provide convenient access to the park. Visitors should note that the historic locomotive (Engine No. 168) was moved for restoration in 2016. Seasonal restrooms are generally available from May through October. (Trails and Open Space Coalition, The Colorado Sun)
attractions.nearby: The Antlers Hotel, Pikes Peak Center for the Performing Arts, Penrose Main Library, AdAmAn Alley, Plaza of the Rockies (Questo, Wanderboat AI)

attractions.name: Colorado Springs Philharmonic
attractions.description: The Colorado Springs Philharmonic is an independent symphony orchestra dedicated to offering brilliant musical performances and in-person experiences that foster community connection. Founded as the successor to the Colorado Springs Symphony, it presents a wide range of classical, pops, and family-friendly concerts throughout its seasonal calendar.
attractions.address: 190 S Cascade Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903-2211
attractions.region: Downtown Colorado Springs / Pikes Peak Region
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 10:00 am – 6:00 pm (Pikes Peak Center Box Office); Saturday: 10:00 am – 4:00 pm; Sunday: Closed (except performance days). Performance times vary.
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Box Office); Event-based (Concerts)
attractions.admission: Tickets typically start at $40 to $65 depending on the performance and venue, with average prices around $89. A 'Free for Kids' program is available for select concerts, allowing up to two children (ages 7-17) to attend for free with the purchase of one full-priced adult ticket.
highlights: Performs at the Pikes Peak Center for the Performing Arts and the Ent Center for the Arts.
Offers a 'Free for Kids' program to nurture the next generation of music lovers.
Features diverse programming including Masterworks, Philharmonic Pops, and Signature Series.
Celebrated its Centennial Season in 2026-27 with special commemorative events.
Led by Music Director Chloé Dufresne as of the 2025-2026 season.
Host to the 'American Soundtrack Festival' celebrating 250 years of American music.
attractions.tips: Order your intermission drinks before the performance starts to have them waiting for you at a reserved table. Season tickets offer the best value for frequent attendees. Students and military members can often find discounted ticket options. Be sure to arrive at least 30 minutes early to enjoy pre-concert talks.
attractions.nearby: U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Museum, Garden of the Gods Park, Pikes Peak Heritage Center, Pioneers Museum.

attractions.name: Colorado_College
attractions.description: Colorado College is a prestigious private liberal arts college known for its innovative Block Plan, where students focus on one subject at a time. Founded in 1874, the campus is set against the backdrop of Pikes Peak and emphasizes creativity, sustainability, and global engagement. About CC

attractions.admission: Free for campus visits; Museum admission (Fine Arts Center): Adults $10, Seniors/Military/Teachers $8, K-12 $5, Pre-K and College Students with ID Free. Fine Arts Center Tickets

Carbon-neutral Tutt Library

attractions.name: Judge Baldwin's Brewing Company
attractions.description: Judge Baldwin's Brewing Company is a legendary local institution and Colorado Springs' first microbrewery, originally opened in 1991 inside the historic Antlers Hotel. Known for its extensive selection of craft beers and hearty pub fare, it remains a celebrated part of the city's downtown dining history and is famously rumored to be haunted by a mustachioed male entity.
attractions.address: 4 S. Cascade Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
attractions.hours: Daily 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM (Pub menu until 12:00 AM)
attractions.days_open: 7 days a week
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant/Bar)
highlights: First microbrewery in Colorado Springs history (est. 1991)
Famous for serving "Yards" and "Half-Yards" of craft beer
Home to local ghost legends including a resident mustachioed entity
Located within the historic Antlers hotel in the heart of downtown
Recipient of the "Best Beer Presentation" award in its early years
Features a wide selection of 50+ draft beers in its current lobby bar iteration
attractions.tips: Be sure to ask the staff about the hotel's famous ghost stories; validate your parking if you use the hotel garage; and try a flight of local Colorado beers to experience the city's craft roots.
attractions.nearby: Pikes Peak, US Olympic & Paralympic Museum, Acacia Park, Garden of the Gods, Colorado Springs Pioneers Museum

It offers three floors of hospitality, featuring a full-service restaurant, a classic billiards hall, and spacious event areas. Phantom Canyon Brewing Co. Official Site

attractions.name: Pikes Peak Center for the Performing Arts (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Pikes Peak Center for the Performing Arts is a premier concert auditorium in downtown Colorado Springs, opened in 1982 as a regional cultural and educational hub. Renowned for its world-class acoustics and design, it serves as the home of the Colorado Springs Philharmonic and hosts over 200 events annually, ranging from Broadway tours to contemporary concerts (Wikipedia, Official Website).
attractions.address: 190 South Cascade Avenue, Colorado Springs, CO 80903-2211 (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday: Closed (Box Office Hours) (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday – Saturday (Box Office); Event-based for performances (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; tickets are typically available through AXS (Official Website)
highlights: Home of the Colorado Springs Philharmonic Orchestra (Official Website)
Internationally acclaimed for its superior acoustical design and engineering (Wikipedia)
Versatile seating capacity of approximately 2,000 across orchestra, mezzanine, and balcony levels (Wikipedia)
Major stop for national Broadway tours and world-renowned musical artists (Official Website)
Key landmark in the economic revitalization of downtown Colorado Springs (Official Website)
attractions.tips: The venue is a cashless facility; only credit/debit cards are accepted for concessions. Use the AXS mobile app for digital ticket entry and arrive early to allow time for security screening and bag checks (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Colorado Springs Pioneers Museum, America the Beautiful Park, U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Museum, and various downtown dining options (Visit Colorado Springs)

attractions.name: Poor Richard's Restaurant
attractions.description: Poor Richard's is a historic Colorado Springs destination that has been a local favorite since 1975, featuring a unique complex that houses a restaurant, bookstore, toy store, and wine bar under one roof. The establishment is known for its New York-style pizza, fresh salads, and a community-focused atmosphere that welcomes families, readers, and diners alike. Poor Richard's Downtown, SpringsMag

attractions.tips: Visit the back patio for a quieter dining experience, and explore the interconnected bookstore and toy store while waiting for your meal. Reservations are recommended for busy nights. Poor Richard's Downtown, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Tejon Street Downtown (Visit Colorado Springs)
attractions.category: Landmark, Shopping, Dining, and Nightlife District
attractions.description: Tejon Street is the main commercial thoroughfare and "vibrant hub" of Downtown Colorado Springs, known for its eclectic mix of independent shops, restaurants, and bars. It serves as the primary artery for the city's downtown core, featuring historic Victorian architecture alongside modern revitalized spaces with wide, pedestrian-friendly sidewalks. (GPSmyCity, Colorado Fun Guide)
attractions.address: Tejon Street, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
attractions.hours: Street: Open 24/7; Downtown Partnership Office (111 S. Tejon St, Ste 703): Monday-Thursday, 9 AM - 4 PM (Downtown Partnership)
attractions.days_open: Daily (GPSmyCity)
attractions.admission: Free (Downtown Partnership)
highlights: Main commercial artery and "vibrant hub" of Downtown Colorado Springs (GPSmyCity)
Home to over 180 independent shops, restaurants, and services (Downtown Partnership)
Features historic Victorian-era buildings and modern public art installations (Colorado Fun Guide)
Recently revitalized with 10-foot wide pedestrian-friendly sidewalks and expanded outdoor dining space (Construction Owners)
Host to major community events like the Festival of Lights Parade (Wikipedia)
Central location with proximity to key landmarks like the Pioneers Museum and Acacia Park (Colorado Fun Guide)
attractions.tips: Explore the "Poor Richard's" complex for a unique mix of a restaurant, toy store, wine bar, and bookstore. For affordable parking, use the downtown city garages which cost roughly $1 per hour. Don't miss hidden underground spots like The Rabbit Hole or Bloom Ultra Lounge for a unique nightlife experience. (Colorado Fun Guide, Downtown Partnership)
attractions.nearby: Colorado Springs Pioneers Museum, Acacia Park, Colorado Springs Fine Arts Center at Colorado College, United States Olympic & Paralympic Museum (Colorado Fun Guide, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: United States Olympic & Paralympic Museum
attractions.description: The United States Olympic & Paralympic Museum is a 60,000-square-foot facility dedicated to the history and achievements of Team USA athletes. It features 12 immersive galleries, interactive sports experiences, and is recognized as one of the most accessible and interactive museums in the world. Official Website

Value Days (Mon-Thu): Adult: $27.95, Child: $15.95, College: $16.95, Seniors/Heroes: $22.95. Winter pricing is lower (e.g., Adult $23.95 Peak / $21.95 Value). Official Website
highlights: Interactive Athlete Training Gallery (sprinting, skiing, archery, etc.)

attractions.name: Colorado Mountain Brewery - Roundhouse
attractions.description: Colorado Mountain Brewery is a premier destination offering "The True Taste of Colorado" through its handcrafted beers and unique Colorado-inspired cuisine. Founded by a U.S. Air Force Academy graduate, the brewery emphasizes the state's rich history and traditions in both its atmosphere and its award-winning menu items.
attractions.address: 600 S 21st St Suite 180
attractions.hours: Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:30 PM
attractions.days_open: Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Located in a historic 14-stall railroad roundhouse built in 1887 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Features award-winning craft beers such as the Ole '59er Amber Ale and Panther IPA, brewed on-site.
Offers unique "Colorado Cuisine" including the Bison Meatloaf Wellington, which was praised by chef Gordon Ramsay.
Boasts expansive outdoor patios with fire pits that provide beautiful views of the Front Range and Colorado sunsets.
Decorated with history-rich elements including a 1902 railroad turnaround photo and Van Briggle pottery pieces.
Veteran-owned and operated, with many menu names and themes giving nods to the U.S. Air Force Academy.
attractions.tips: Take advantage of Happy Hour specials available Sunday through Friday from 3 PM to 6 PM, and late-night Sunday through Thursday from 8 PM to close. For a bit of history, look for the historic railroad turntable that still exists under the pavement of the front parking lot. Reservations are generally not needed for the Roundhouse location but are recommended for larger parties.

attractions.name: Ghost Town Museum Official Website
attractions.description: Ghost Town Museum is an indoor preservation of Colorado's pioneer past, featuring authentic 19th-century buildings and artifacts rescued from the Pikes Peak region. Created in 1954, it offers an immersive Wild West experience where visitors can explore a reconstructed town, participate in hands-on historical activities, and learn about the gold mining era. Official Website, Visit Colorado Springs
attractions.address: 400 S 21st St, Colorado Springs, CO 80904 Official Website, Pikes Peak Region Attractions
attractions.region: Old Colorado City / West Side Wonderful Museums, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: June–August: Daily 10:00 a.m. – 6:00 p.m.; September–May: Daily 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Last admission 30 minutes before closing). Official Website
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round (closed New Year’s Day, Easter, Thanksgiving, and Christmas). Official Website

The museum is located indoors and is climate-controlled, making it a great destination for snow days or hot summer afternoons. Visit during the summer months (May-September) to enjoy the gold panning activity included with admission. Tripadvisor, Pikes Peak Region Attractions

Visitors can enjoy a variety of slot machines, table games, and fine dining while exploring the town's rich mining heritage.
attractions.address: 337 E. Bennett Ave (Heritage Center), Cripple Creek, CO 80813

attractions.days_open: Daily

highlights: Over 10 unique casinos located within a National Historic Landmark district
Wild donkeys roaming the streets, descendants of the original mine animals
Historic Victorian architecture and preserved 19th-century storefronts
Mollie Kathleen Gold Mine tours descending 1,000 feet underground nearby
Cripple Creek & Victor Narrow Gauge Railroad offering scenic mountain trips
Breathtaking high-altitude views at an elevation of 9,494 feet
attractions.tips: Stay hydrated to avoid altitude sickness at nearly 9,500 feet, and always bring layers as mountain weather changes rapidly. Use the free city shuttle to travel between different casinos and attractions throughout the district. Please note that you must be 21 or older to enter casino gaming areas.

attractions.name: Cripple Creek Historic District
attractions.description: The Cripple Creek Historic District is a National Historic Landmark that preserves the legacy of one of the world's most productive gold mining regions. Established in the 1890s, the district encompasses the towns of Cripple Creek and Victor, featuring well-preserved Victorian architecture, historic mines, and a wealth of museums and trails. Today, it offers a blend of Old West history, scenic mountain beauty, and modern attractions like casinos and historic train rides.
attractions.address: 9283 S. Highway 67 (Heritage Center), Cripple Creek, CO 80813
attractions.hours: Cripple Creek Heritage Center: Daily 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; District Museum: Summer 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM Daily, Winter Sat-Sun 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM.
attractions.days_open: Daily (District is accessible 24/7; visitor centers and museums vary by season).
attractions.admission: Free (Individual museums and attractions like the Narrow Gauge Railroad or mine tours have separate fees, typically ranging from $5 to $20+).
highlights: Cripple Creek & Victor Narrow Gauge Railroad (45-minute scenic steam train tour)
Mollie Kathleen Gold Mine (descent 1,000 feet underground for a historic mine tour)
Cripple Creek Heritage Center (state-of-the-art visitor center with hands-on exhibits)
Outlaws and Lawmen Jail Museum (housed in the original 1901 red brick jail)
Vindicator Valley Trail (a "Trail of Gold" through historic mine ruins)
Victor Lowell Thomas Museum (artifacts from the gold rush and memorabilia of the famous broadcast journalist)
attractions.tips: The district sits at an elevation of over 9,000 feet, so stay hydrated and be mindful of high-altitude symptoms. Keep an eye out for the free-roaming herd of donkeys in Cripple Creek, descendants of those used in the mines. Late September is the best time for fall foliage along the scenic Gold Belt Byway. Check seasonal schedules as many outdoor attractions close for winter.
attractions.nearby: Pikes Peak, Florissant Fossil Beds National Monument, Mueller State Park, Eleven Mile State Park, Skaguay Reservoir.

attractions.name: Florissant Fossil Beds National Monument
attractions.description: Florissant Fossil Beds National Monument preserves one of the richest and most diverse fossil deposits in the world, featuring massive petrified redwood stumps and thousands of detailed fossils of insects and plants. This 34-million-year-old Eocene ecosystem offers a unique glimpse into prehistoric Colorado through its fossilized remains and scenic mountain landscapes. (National Park Service)
attractions.address: 15807 Teller County Road 1, Florissant, CO 80816
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: $10.00 per person (ages 16 and older), Free for children 15 and under, $35.00 Annual Park Pass (National Park Service)
highlights: Massive petrified redwood stumps, including the 'Big Stump' (National Park Service)
Detailed fossils of insects, spiders, and leaves in paper shale (National Park Service)
14 miles of nature trails including the Petrified Forest Loop and Ponderosa Loop (National Park Service)
The 1878 Hornbek Homestead historic site (National Park Service)
State-of-the-art Visitor Center with hands-on exhibits and park film (Visit Colorado Springs)
Junior Ranger program for kids to earn badges (National Park Service)
attractions.tips: The monument is located at 8,500 feet altitude, so visitors should pace themselves and stay hydrated; pets are limited to the parking lot and specific pet exercise areas; summer visitors should check for ranger-led 'Hikes for Your Health' and star programs (National Park Service, Visit Colorado Springs)
attractions.nearby: Colorado Wolf and Wildlife Center, Rocky Mountain Dinosaur Resource Center, Cripple Creek, Pikes Peak, Royal Gorge Bridge & Park (Florissant Fossil Quarry)

attractions.name: Manitou Cliff Dwellings (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Manitou Cliff Dwellings is a unique historical attraction featuring relocated Ancestral Puebloan ruins reassembled from McElmo Canyon in southwestern Colorado. Visitors can walk through 40 unique rooms and explore two museums housed in a traditional Pueblo-style building that showcase authentic pottery, tools, and artifacts. (Official Website, Pikes Peak Region Attractions)
attractions.address: 10 Cliff Road, Manitou Springs, CO 80829 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: May–August: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; March: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; April, September, October: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; November: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; December–February: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week), weather permitting; closed Thanksgiving and Christmas (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults (12 and over): $12.75 + Tax; Children (4-11): $8.00 + Tax; Children (3 & under): Free; Seniors (60 plus): $10.00 + Tax; Anyone over 100: Free; Military: $10.75 (Official Website, Pikes Peak Region Attractions)
highlights: Walk through and touch 40 authentic Ancestral Puebloan rooms relocated from McElmo Canyon (Pikes Peak Region Attractions)
Explore the Ancestral Puebloan Museum featuring dioramas, pottery, weapons, and tools (Official Website)
Visit the Pueblo-style building constructed in traditional Taos Indian style (Official Website)
Experience a scenic nature walk with well-labeled native Colorado flowers and plants (Pikes Peak Region Attractions)
Browse the large gift shop for Native American-made jewelry, crafts, and souvenirs (Official Website)
Learn about indigenous cultures through informative videos and educational displays (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for exploring the rocky dwellings. Pets are welcome throughout the ruins and museum if kept on a leash. Note that while the museum and sidewalk are accessible, the dwellings themselves involve narrow passages and stairs that are not wheelchair or stroller friendly. (Official Website, Pikes Peak Region Attractions)

attractions.name: Manitou Incline
attractions.description: The Manitou Incline is an extreme hiking trail consisting of 2,768 steps that climb nearly 2,000 vertical feet in less than a mile. Originally built as a cable car track to transport materials for Pikes Peak pipelines, it now serves as a premier fitness challenge with grades as steep as 68%. City of Colorado Springs, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Hydro Street (behind the Pikes Peak Cog Railway), Manitou Springs, CO 80829
attractions.region: Colorado Springs Area / Pikes Peak Region
attractions.hours: Summer (May 1 – Oct 31): 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Winter (Nov 1 – Apr 30): 6:00 AM - 3:00 PM City of Colorado Springs
attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free (Online reservation and signed waiver required) City of Colorado Springs
attractions.phone: (719) 385-5940 (Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation & Cultural Services) Trails and Open Space Coalition
highlights: 2,768 steps made of railroad ties
Nearly 2,000 feet of vertical elevation gain in 0.88 miles
Steepest grade of 68% and average grade of 45%
Panoramic views of the Pikes Peak region and Manitou Springs
Historical significance as a former funicular railway track
Connection to the scenic Barr Trail for the descent
attractions.tips: Make a reservation well in advance as spots fill quickly. Bring at least 1-2 liters of water and acclimate to the altitude before attempting. Do not descend the steps; use the Barr Trail for a safer 3-mile downhill hike. Utilize the free Route 33 shuttle from the Dillon Mobility Hub or Hiawatha Gardens to reach the trailhead. City of Colorado Springs, Visit Colorado Springs

attractions.name: Manitou Springs Historic District
attractions.description: Manitou Springs Historic District is a resort community known for its natural mineral springs and stunning geographic location at the base of Pikes Peak. Established in the late 19th century, it features a collection of Victorian-style buildings, historic inns, and the famous mineral springs that once served as a health resort for tuberculosis sufferers. It is one of the largest National Historic Districts in the United States, preserving much of its 19th-century charm through stone-walled terraces and winding hillside streets (Wikipedia, Manitou Springs History).
attractions.address: Manitou Avenue (roughly bounded by US 24, Ruxton Avenue, El Paso Boulevard and Iron Mountain Avenue)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Visitor Center Hours) (Pikes Peak Bulletin, Colorado.com)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Pikes Peak Bulletin)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor, Manitou Springs Heritage Center)
highlights: Mineral Springs Walking Tour: Visit eight public cold-water drinking springs scattered throughout the district (Visit Manitou Springs).
Historic Architecture: Features diverse styles including Queen Anne, Gothic Revival, and Victorian Shingle architecture (Manitou Springs Design Guidelines).
Miramont Castle: A historic museum and Victorian mansion built in 1895 with an eclectic mix of architectural styles (Miramont Castle).
Manitou Heritage Center: A local museum dedicated to preserving and interpreting the history and culture of the Pikes Peak region (Manitou Springs Heritage Museum).
The Incline: A famous former cable tramway route that is now a challenging 2,744-step hiking trail (Wikipedia).
Emma Crawford Coffin Races: A quirky annual event commemorating a local resident who was buried atop Red Mountain (Visit Manitou Springs).
attractions.tips: Stop by the Manitou Springs Visitor Center at 354 Manitou Ave to pick up a free tasting cup and a map for the mineral springs walking tour (Pikes Peak Region Attractions). Wear comfortable walking shoes, as many streets are narrow and meander up steep hillsides (Manitou Springs Design Guidelines). Parking can be limited in the historic core, so consider using the free city shuttles (Visit Manitou Springs).
attractions.nearby: Garden of the Gods, Cave of the Winds Mountain Park, Manitou Cliff Dwellings, Pikes Peak, Old Colorado City (Visit Colorado Springs)

Established in 1872 as a scenic health resort, the town is a certified Colorado Creative District known for its vibrant arts scene, historic architecture, and outdoor adventures like the Manitou Incline.

attractions.name: Rampart Range Road (NFSR 300)
attractions.description: Rampart Range Road is a historic 60-mile scenic dirt road that winds through the Pike National Forest between Colorado Springs and Sedalia. Built by the Civilian Conservation Corps in the 1930s, the road offers breathtaking panoramic views of Pikes Peak and the Front Range. It serves as a popular hub for off-highway vehicle (OHV) enthusiasts, dispersed campers, and hikers exploring the rugged terrain of the Rampart Range. USDA Forest Service, Wikipedia
attractions.address: NFSR 300 (access via Garden of the Gods Park)
attractions.region: Pikes Peak Region / West Colorado Springs Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Note: Trailheads and OHV staging areas are open sunrise to sunset) USDA Forest Service

attractions.admission: Free (Fees apply for developed and designated dispersed camping) USDA Forest Service, RRMMC

There is no potable water or trash service; visitors must pack out all waste and bring their own supplies. Always check for seasonal closures and fire restrictions before visiting. USDA Forest Service, RRMMC

attractions.name: The Broadmoor Seven Falls
attractions.description: The Broadmoor Seven Falls is a magnificent series of seven cascading waterfalls located in a 1,250-foot-walled box canyon between the towering Pillars of Hercules. It is the only waterfall in Colorado to be included on National Geographic's list of International Waterfalls and is famously known as 'The Grandest Mile of Scenery in Colorado.' Visitors can experience the falls from the base, climb 224 steps to the top, or take an in-mountain elevator to the Eagle’s Nest observation platform. (Seven Falls, Visit Colorado Springs)

attractions.name: Williams Canyon (home to Cave of the Winds Mountain Park)
attractions.description: Williams Canyon is a spectacular, narrow limestone canyon located at the base of Pikes Peak, featuring towering 200-foot walls and unique geological formations. It is home to the famous Cave of the Winds Mountain Park and offers a variety of experiences ranging from guided cave explorations to above-ground adventure attractions and rugged hiking trails. The canyon has a rich history, having been a popular sight-seeing destination since the 1870s and serving as the waterway responsible for significant historical floods in the area. Visit Manitou Springs, Outdoor Explorer

attractions.hours: Summer (Memorial Day - Labor Day): 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Last tour at 8:00 PM); Winter (September - May): 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Last tour at 4:00 PM). The road is famous for its hand-cut rock tunnels and panoramic mountain views, though a significant portion is closed to vehicle traffic due to a 1988 tunnel collapse, remaining popular for hiking and biking.

attractions.region: Southwest Colorado Springs / Pike National Forest

attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Nine historic tunnels hand-cut into the granite mountainside
Breathtaking panoramic views of the Pikes Peak region and Colorado Springs
Access to popular hiking trails like Seven Bridges and St. Mary’s Falls
Fascinating history as the former "Short Line" railroad route
Stunning fall foliage displays as the mountain aspens turn gold

attractions.tips: A high-clearance vehicle is recommended for the unpaved upper sections due to potholes and washboarding. Bring plenty of water and supplies as there are no services on the road. It serves as a great spot for families, beginner hikers, and visitors looking to enjoy the natural beauty of the Rocky Mountains. Official Site, Garden of the Gods Colorado

Originally constructed as a carriage road in the early 1900s, it now serves as a popular destination for hikers and mountain bikers, featuring dramatic shelf-road sections and tunnels carved directly through granite.

Scenic high-altitude vantage points reaching nearly 9,000 feet in elevation.

attractions.name: Memorial Park

It serves as a premier destination for sports and recreation, featuring the Mark \"Pa\" Sertich Ice Center, the 7-Eleven US Olympic Velodrome, and Colorado's second-largest skate park. The park is also a major cultural hub, famously hosting the annual Labor Day Lift Off hot air balloon festival and various memorials dedicated to fallen service members and firefighters (Visit Colorado Springs, Trails and Open Space Coalition).

Mark \\"Pa\\" Sertich Ice Center: An indoor arena providing public skating and hockey sessions year-round (Visit Colorado Springs).
Memorial Park Skate Park: A 40,000-square-foot competition-size facility for skateboarders, BMX riders, and inline skaters (Visit Colorado Springs).
Fallen Fire Fighter Memorial: An international memorial honoring firefighters from the US and Canada who died in the line of duty (Trails and Open Space Coalition).
7-Eleven US Olympic Velodrome: A top-tier bicycle racing track that is a centerpiece for local and national cycling (Wikipedia).
Labor Day Lift Off: A massive annual festival featuring sunrise hot air balloon launches and evening balloon glows (Beaton Brothers).

attractions.description: Stargazers Theatre & Event Center is a unique, mid-century modern dome building that originally opened in 1969 as a United Artists Cinerama 150 movie theatre. Reopened in 2009 after extensive renovations, it now serves as a premier live performance venue in southern Colorado, hosting over 150 events annually including concerts, film screenings, and community gatherings. Stargazers Theatre, Uncover Colorado

attractions.name: Templeton Gap Trail
attractions.description: The Templeton Gap Trail is a vital 4.2-mile multi-use urban corridor in central Colorado Springs that follows the Templeton Gap Floodway. It serves as a key recreational and transportation link, connecting major parks like Palmer Park and Goose Gossage Youth Sports Complex to the Pikes Peak Greenway. The trail features a mostly paved surface of concrete and asphalt, winding through diverse neighborhoods and offering unique views of the city's flood control infrastructure. (Trails and Open Space Coalition), (Wikipedia)

attractions.region: Central / Northeast Colorado Springs (Venetian Village area) (Wikipedia)

attractions.phone: 719-385-5940 (Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services) (City of Colorado Springs)

attractions.tips: Best accessed from the Goose Gossage Youth Sports Complex or Portal Park for dedicated parking. Be prepared for several street crossings, including major intersections like North Nevada Avenue and Union Boulevard. Sections of the trail are highly exposed, so bringing water and sun protection is recommended for summer visits. (Trails and Open Space Coalition), (Komoot)

attractions.name: The Blue Star
attractions.description: The Blue Star is a celebrated Colorado Springs culinary institution, originally established in 1995 and known for its eclectic, upscale, and approachable elevated cuisine. After closing in 2017, it is being reborn as a "2.0 version" in the Ivywild neighborhood, continuing its legacy as a gastronomic staple with a focus on fresh, local ingredients and a from-scratch kitchen. The restaurant is currently undergoing renovations with a grand reopening anticipated in the third quarter of 2026 (The Blue Star, Springsmag).

attractions.region: Ivywild Neighborhood / Downtown (The Blue Star, TableAgent)

attractions.name: Western Museum of Mining & Industry (WMMI Website)
attractions.description: The Western Museum of Mining and Industry (WMMI) is a unique and interactive museum dedicated to educating the public about the importance of mining and industry by preserving and interpreting the rich history of the American West. Located on a 27-acre historic campus, it features working machinery, a model mining drift, and gold panning stations that bring Colorado's heritage to life through hands-on science and towering machinery (WMMI Website, Pikes Peak Attractions).
